en: A PSALM OF ASAPH.Truly God is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart.
zh: 神實在善待以色列， 善待那些內心清潔的人。

en: But as for me, my feet had almost stumbled, my steps had nearly slipped.
zh: 至於我，我的腳幾乎滑跌， 我 險些跌倒。

en: For I was envious of the arrogant when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
zh: 我看見惡人興隆， 我就嫉妒狂傲的人。

en: For they have no pangs until death; their bodies are fat and sleek.
zh: 他們沒有痛苦， 他們的身體又健康又肥壯 。

en: They are not in trouble as others are; they are not stricken like the rest of mankind.
zh: 他們沒有一般人所受的苦難， 也不像普通人一樣遭遇災害。

en: Therefore pride is their necklace; violence covers them as a garment.
zh: 所以，驕傲像鍊子戴在他們的頸項上， 強暴好像衣裳穿在他們的身上。

en: Their eyes swell out through fatness; their hearts overflow with follies.
zh: 他們的罪孽是出於麻木的心 ， 他們心裡的惡念氾濫。

en: They scoff and speak with malice; loftily they threaten oppression.
zh: 他們譏笑人，懷著惡意說欺壓人的話，他們說話自高。

en: They set their mouths against the heavens, and their tongue struts through the earth.
zh: 他們用口褻瀆上天， 他們用舌頭毀謗全地。

en: Therefore his people turn back to them, and find no fault in them.
zh: 因此他的人民歸回那裡去， 並且喝光了大量的水。

en: And they say, "How can God know? Is there knowledge in the Most High?"
zh: 他們說：“ 神怎會曉得？ 至高者有知識嗎？”

en: Behold, these are the wicked; always at ease, they increase in riches.
zh: 看這些惡人， 他們常享安逸，財富卻增加。

en: All in vain have I kept my heart clean and washed my hands in innocence.
zh: 我謹守我心純潔實在徒然； 我洗手表明清白也是枉然。

en: For all the day long I have been stricken and rebuked every morning.
zh: 因為我終日受傷害， 每天早晨受懲罰。

en: If I had said, "I will speak thus," I would have betrayed the generation of your children.
zh: 如果我心裡說：“我要說這樣的話”， 我就是對你這一代的眾兒女不忠了。

en: But when I thought how to understand this, it seemed to me a wearisome task,
zh: 我思想要明白這事， 我就看為煩惱；

en: until I went into the sanctuary of God; then I discerned their end.
zh: 直到我進了 神的聖所， 才明白他們的結局。

en: Truly you set them in slippery places; you make them fall to ruin.
zh: 你實在把他們安放在滑地， 使他們倒下、滅亡。

en: How they are destroyed in a moment, swept away utterly by terrors!
zh: 他們忽然間成了多麼荒涼， 被突然的驚恐完全消滅。

en: Like a dream when one awakes, O Lord, when you rouse yourself, you despise them as phantoms.
zh: 人睡醒了怎樣看夢， 主啊！你睡醒了，也要照樣輕看他們 。

en: When my soul was embittered, when I was pricked in heart,
zh: 我心中酸苦， 我肺腑刺痛的時候，

en: I was brutish and ignorant; I was like a beast toward you.
zh: 我是愚昧無知的； 我在你面前就像畜類一般。

en: Nevertheless, I am continually with you; you hold my right hand.
zh: 但是，我仍常與你同在； 你緊握著我的右手。

en: You guide me with your counsel, and afterward you will receive me to glory.
zh: 你要以你的訓言引領我， 以後還要接我到榮耀裡去。

en: Whom have I in heaven but you? And there is nothing on earth that I desire besides you.
zh: 除你以外，在天上，我還有誰呢？ 除你以外，在地上，我也無所愛慕。

en: My flesh and my heart may fail, but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.
zh: 我的肉身和我的內心雖然漸漸衰弱， 神卻永遠是我心裡的磐石，是我的業分。

en: For behold, those who are far from you shall perish; you put an end to everyone who is unfaithful to you.
zh: 看哪！遠離你的，必定滅亡； 凡是對你不貞的，你都要滅絕。

en: But for me it is good to be near God; I have made the Lord GOD my refuge, that I may tell of all your works.
zh: 對我來說，親近 神是美好的， 我以主耶和華為我的避難所； 我要述說你的一切作為。
